commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)

- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

commit.message:
fix: reset TMP/TEMP/TMPDIR to /tmp in default.sh

Nix sets TMP/TEMP/NIX_BUILD_TOP to a build-time scratch dir under
/private/tmp/nix-build-nix-shell.drv-N/ that gets cleaned up after
build. The old code only unset TMPDIR, leaving TMP and TEMP pointing
to the deleted directory. This caused Claude Code (and any tool using
$TMP for temp files) to fail with "No such file or directory" after
every command, producing false exit code 1.

Now explicitly export all three to /tmp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: llm
repository.description: LLM/AI documentation and guides for R package development workflows

commit.message:
feat(anam): server-side custom LLM endpoint — fixes "can't hear me" bug

Replaces CUSTOMER_CLIENT_V1 (broken client-side round-trip) with a
server-side custom LLM registered with Anam. Anam servers now call
our /v1/chat/completions endpoint directly (server-to-server).

Architecture:
  Anam STT → Anam servers → POST /v1/chat/completions (our endpoint)
  → Parse ASPIRE_CTX from system prompt (suite_id, user_id, agent)
  → Fetch user profile from suite_profiles
  → Forward to LangGraph orchestrator (/v1/intents)
  → Stream response back in OpenAI Chat Completions SSE format
  → Anam TTS → Avatar speaks

Changes:
- New /v1/chat/completions endpoint with shared secret auth
- Session creation embeds [ASPIRE_CTX:...] in systemPrompt
- Persona configs use ANAM_CUSTOM_LLM_ID instead of CUSTOMER_CLIENT_V1
- Added ANAM_LLM_SECRET and ANAM_CUSTOM_LLM_ID to secrets mapping
- Registered custom LLM with Anam API (ID: 5b9a772b-9b20-41bb-a0c4-7f691c109a43)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use --mode=skip-build to bypass lavamoat allow-scripts plugin on Vercel

The allow-scripts plugin registers a postinstall hook that fails in Vercel.
--mode=skip-build skips all lifecycle scripts including plugin hooks.

Dynamic chat bar placeholder based on onboarding state

Chat bar placeholder text now reflects the user's onboarding state:
- Observing: "I'm learning how you work — ask me what I've noticed..."
- Ready: "I have suggestions for organizing your journal — let's review"
- Interviewing: "Tell me about your work..."
- Default/complete/skipped: "Send a message..."

Implemented via Flask context processor in convey/apps.py — reads
awareness state and injects chat_bar_placeholder into all templates.
Template uses Jinja2 variable with safe fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete attachment support for both Microsoft and JMAP

Fixed all 4 attachment issues:

1. Email detail view: Attachments now clickable/downloadable
   - Added onClick handler to attachment buttons
   - Fetches and downloads attachment on click

2. JMAP attachment download: Full support implemented
   - Updated download API to detect JMAP vs Microsoft accounts
   - JMAP downloads use blob API: {apiUrl}/download/{accountId}/{blobId}/{name}
   - Added blobId to attachment metadata during sync
   - Both delta and full sync now store blobId

3. JMAP send with attachments: Complete implementation
   - Upload attachments as blobs first to get blobIds
   - Create Email/set with attachment references
   - Create EmailSubmission/set to send
   - Works alongside existing Microsoft send logic

4. Inline preview: Images and PDFs open in modal
   - Images: Show full-size inline
   - PDFs: iframe viewer
   - Other files: Download as before
   - Modal has close button and download option

Changes:
- app/api/mail/attachments/[messageId]/[attachmentId]/route.ts: JMAP download support
- app/api/mail/send/route.ts: JMAP send with attachments
- components/inbox/EmailReadClient.tsx: Clickable attachments + preview modal
- lib/providers/jmap/JmapEmailProvider.ts: Store blobId in attachments

All providers now have full attachment support.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase build concurrency to speed up manifest fetching

Raise CANOPY_FETCH_CONCURRENCY from 1 to 10 and CANOPY_CHUNK_SIZE
from 10 to 25. Sequential fetching of 100 manifests was the primary
cause of the 207s build time.

* feat: enterprise hardening — logging, error handling, signal handlers, input validation

CRITICAL fixes from enterprise audit (6.5→8.5/10):

1. agent.ts: try-catch wrapper around query loop + 30-min wall-clock timeout
   + new 'error' event type in DiscoveryEvent union
2. logger.ts: structured JSON logging to stderr (ELK/Datadog/Splunk compatible)
   - logInfo, logError, logWarn, logDebug with context objects
   - verbose mode for DEBUG filtering
3. cli.ts: graceful shutdown via SIGTERM/SIGINT signal handlers
   - DB cleanup on forced termination
   - Input validation for --depth (1–50) and --max-turns (1–500)
   - Structured logging at discovery start/end/error
   - Version bump to 0.9.3
4. platform.ts: safeEnv() — whitelist env vars for child processes
   - Prevents credential leakage (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY etc.)
5. index.ts: export logger functions in public API

Tests: 210 passed (12 files) — +10 new tests for logger

* harden: add Zod validation to all DB row mappers

All 7 row mappers (session, node, edge, event, task, workflow, connection)
now validate raw SQLite rows through Zod schemas before mapping to
TypeScript types. Catches corrupted or schema-mismatched data at read time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017YUJ3jA5AtNqkGv8P3QKWv

* harden: add circuit breaker for cloud CLI scan tools

Cloud scan tools (k8s, AWS, GCP, Azure) now use a circuit breaker that
skips remaining commands after 3 consecutive failures within a single
scan. Prevents long hangs when a cloud CLI is unconfigured or rate-limited.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix macOS VM boot: SSH over vsock end-to-end (#87)

* Fix macOS VM boot: SSH over vsock now works end-to-end

Root causes found and fixed:
- Kernel decompression: Alpine ARM64 vmlinuz is a PE32+ EFI stub, not plain
  gzip. Extract compressed kernel from PE using gzip magic byte search.
- Kernel cmdline: add rootfstype=ext4 and init=/entrypoint.sh
- vsock socket path: use /tmp/ instead of ~/Library/Application Support/
  (vfkit cannot handle spaces in socketURL paths)
- vsock mode: use ,connect (creates host Unix socket for incoming connections)
- Entrypoint: remount root rw, bring up loopback, DHCP for internet,
  modprobe vmw_vsock_virtio_transport, start sshd before socat
- SSH command: remove sh -c wrapper (SSH already runs through shell)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump version to 0.1.58

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify: remove redundant ssh pubkey install, improve entrypoint

- Remove ssh_run that installed authorized_keys over SSH (redundant with
  virtio-fs mount, and had shell injection surface via pubkey interpolation)
- Background DHCP so it doesn't block SSH-over-vsock boot path
- Add signal trap for clean PID 1 shutdown
- Use std::env::temp_dir() for vsock socket (per-user on macOS)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix data file CWD mismatch: symlink data dirs when marimo changes CWD

Data files are written to /home/pyodide/ (Pyodide's default CWD) after
loadPyodide, but marimo changes CWD to /tmp/marimo_XX/ before running
notebook code. Relative paths like 'data/scenario.lms' then fail.

Monkey-patch os.chdir in the repo file loader injection to symlink
top-level data directories from the original CWD into the new CWD
whenever marimo (or anything else) changes directory.

commit.message:
Fix fps filter ignored on VAAPI: move after hwdownload

The fps filter is a software filter that can't operate on VAAPI
hardware surfaces. It was silently ignored, causing 130fps output.
Moved fps after hwdownload+format so it runs on system memory frames:
scale_vaapi -> hwdownload -> format=nv12 -> fps=30

commit.message:
feat: add test utilities for TUI integration testing (v1.5.0 3/5)

Implemented MockTerminal and EventSimulator for writing integration
tests without requiring a real TTY. Enables snapshot testing and
event replay for comprehensive TUI widget testing.

MockTerminal features:
- Fake terminal with configurable dimensions
- Event queue management (push/poll)
- Snapshot testing with assertSnapshot()
- Buffer inspection (getChar, getStyle)
- Draw call tracking
- Resize simulation
- No TTY dependency

EventSimulator features:
- Complete keyboard event simulation
- typeString() for character sequences
- Navigation keys (arrows, home, end, page up/down)
- Editing keys (enter, backspace, tab, escape, delete, insert)
- Function keys (F1-F12)
- Modifier support (Ctrl, Alt, Shift)
- Custom event injection
- Resize events

Fixed:
- Zig 0.15.2 ArrayList API compatibility (initCapacity, allocator params)
- UTF-8 encoding in snapshot generation
- .gitignore test_* pattern (now allows test_utils.zig source files)

Tests: 19 new tests (579/581 passing, 2 TTY-dependent skipped)

commit.message:
refactor: decompose BatteryEnvUnified God Object into modular components

Extract 8 components from 3,071-line monolith using Strangler Fig pattern:
- DataPreparer: data validation, rolling stats, precomputed O(1) arrays
- CapacityManager: cascade capacity limits (DAM→aFRR→IDA→mFRR→XBID→FreeBid)
- ObservationBuilder: 70-112 feature observation vector construction
- MaskBuilder: 32/48-bit hierarchical action masks
- DamExecutor: DAM commitment, endogenous schedule, SoC reserve/prediction
- AfrrExecutor: aFRR commitment, selection, activation simulation
- IdaExecutor: IDA gate closure, schedule generation, obs helpers
- TradingExecutor: mFRR, XBID, FreeBid execution

Main env shrinks from 3,071→1,550 lines (50% reduction). Zero breaking
changes — all 262 original tests pass without modification. Fixes O(n)
bug in _check_soc_trajectory_feasible() (was scanning df.index.date).
Adds 50 new component-level tests (312 total).

commit.message:
Reduce MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS to prevent prompt truncation on every query

Logs showed every single query was hitting the head+tail truncation fallback:
  WARN Prompt (3663 tokens) exceeds safe limit (3584)
  WARN Prompt (4311 tokens) exceeds safe limit (3584)

Legal text tokenizes at ~2.5 chars/token, so 10,000 chars → ~4,000 tokens.
With RULES_PROMPT (~200t) + question overhead (~50t), the total consistently
blew past the 3,584 token safe limit, causing middle context chunks to be
dropped and fragmented input to reach the model.

5,500 chars × 2.5 chars/token ≈ 2,200 tokens + 250 overhead = 2,450 total,
well under 3,584 — no truncation, no fragmented context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add mem_runtime for WasmGC, fix method-on-temporary SIGSEGV, arithmetic >> shift

Compiler fixes:
- Arithmetic right shift: >> now maps to SAR (sign-extending) matching Zig
  semantics. Previously mapped to SHR (logical/unsigned).
- Method call on temporary SIGSEGV: chaining like list.get(i).method()
  crashed because the temporary return value had no stable address for
  the implicit self pointer. Fix: spill non-lvalue receivers to a temp
  local in lowerMethodCall, matching the existing pattern for function
  call args (lower.zig line 7676).

Runtime:
- New compiler/codegen/mem_runtime.zig: standalone bump-allocator versions
  of alloc, dealloc, realloc, memcpy, memset_zero, string_eq, string_concat.
  These were previously in arc.zig but are general-purpose stdlib ops needed
  on both ARC and WasmGC paths. Gating ARC out of Wasm (317493c) removed
  them — now they're independent.
- gen.zig: register compound_len_locals for STRING-typed args.

Self-hosted wasm backend:
- assemble.cot (580 lines, 10 tests): Prog→Wasm bytes, LEB128, opcodes
- link.cot (590 lines, 7 tests): Wasm module linker matching Zig reference
  (shared type_storage with offset/len, gc_field_storage, store-then-compare
  type dedup with rollback)
- Simplified SLEB128 in assemble.cot to use plain >> (now arithmetic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

WS4: Set model to opus (was inherit), add maxTurns 50, fix hook
syntax to ${TOOL_INPUT}, add Stop hook for phase completeness.
Team teammates now use model sonnet.

commit.message:
redesign splash: 3D card fan with floating animations and neon title

- 4 cards fanned out with rotateZ + perspective + translateZ depth
- each card bobs independently with staggered float animations
- radial gradient bg with star-field overlay
- title pops in with spring animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
